footer.tagline: Twelve private villas above the Ayung River, Ubud.

meta.title: Uma Sari — Ubud Bali
meta.description: Twelve private villas hidden among Ubud's ancient rice terraces.

hero.title: Where the jungle breathes around you
hero.subtitle: Twelve private villas hidden among Ubud's ancient rice terraces. A place to arrive slowly, and leave differently.

intro.label: Our Philosophy
intro.title: Luxury that leaves no trace
body: Uma Sari was built with one belief: that true luxury is invisible. No grand lobbies. No performance. Just twelve villas, open to the jungle, where the only agenda is your own.
Each villa was designed by local architects using reclaimed teak and volcanic stone — materials that age with the land, not against it.

quote.text: We arrived as tourists. We left as something closer to ourselves.

meta.title: Uma Sari — Villas
meta.description: Twelve private villas above the Ayung River valley in Ubud, Bali.

items.name: The Sanctuary
items.tagline: The highest point of Uma Sari, where the valley opens beneath you
description: The Sanctuary sits at the highest point of Uma Sari, where the Ayung River valley opens beneath you and the jungle rises on all sides. It is our largest villa — three bedrooms, a 20-metre infinity pool, and an open-air living pavilion that makes the distinction between inside and outside irrelevant.

Built by Balinese architect I Wayan Suweca using reclaimed teak from a 200-year-old joglo, every surface here has a story. The stone floors are volcanic. The roof is hand-laid alang-alang grass. The pool appears to pour directly into the valley below.
Guests of The Sanctuary receive a dedicated private butler for the duration of their stay, in-villa breakfast served daily, and priority access to all Uma Sari experiences.

items.name: The Terrace Suite
items.tagline: Wake to rice fields stretching to the horizon
description: The Terrace Suite faces directly east, toward the ancient rice terraces that have been cultivated on this hillside for eight centuries. In the early morning, when the mist lifts and the fields catch the first light, it is the most beautiful view on the property.
Two bedrooms, a private plunge pool, and a garden terrace that extends the living space into the landscape.

items.name: The Forest Villa
items.tagline: Hidden deep in the canopy, private by design
description: The Forest Villa is entirely enclosed by jungle. From the pool deck, you can see nothing but trees — no other villas, no road, no horizon. It is the most private space on the property.
Designed for couples, it has a single king bedroom, an indoor-outdoor bathroom open to the forest, and a pool that sits flush with the surrounding vegetation.

items.name: The Pavilion
items.tagline: An open-air way of living, surrounded by the garden
description: The Pavilion takes its name from its central living space — a completely open-air room with no walls, just a thatched roof and the garden on all sides. In the dry season, the line between living inside and outside disappears entirely.
Two bedrooms, a garden pool, and a kitchen available for in-villa cooking classes.

items.name: The Canopy House
items.tagline: Suspended above the jungle floor, level with the treetops
description: The Canopy House is built on a raised platform that puts you at treetop level — the pool deck sits eight metres above the jungle floor, among the branches rather than beneath them.
Two bedrooms, a treetop pool, and an observation deck that is the best place on the property to watch the sunset.

meta.title: Uma Sari — Experiences
meta.description: 18 curated private experiences in and around Ubud, Bali.

hero.title: Life is the itinerary
hero.subtitle: We don't believe in scheduled fun. Every experience at Uma Sari begins with what you actually want — and we build around that.

stats.label: Curated experiences, from rice terrace walks at dawn to private cooking classes at dusk
Local guides and artisans who have worked with Uma Sari since we opened
Group tours. Every experience is arranged privately, for your party alone

items.name: Jungle Spa at Dawn
items.description: Begin before the rest of the world wakes. Our open-air spa pavilion, set above the river valley, offers traditional Balinese massage and herbal treatments as the mist lifts from the jungle below.

items.name: Market & Kitchen
items.description: Visit Ubud's morning market with our chef, then cook a three-course Balinese feast in our open kitchen.

items.name: Sacred Temple Walk
items.description: A private walking tour of Ubud's oldest temples with a local priest as your guide.

items.name: Sunrise Surf, Canggu
items.description: Our driver picks you up at 5am. You're in the water at Berawa by 6:15, with a private instructor, before the crowds arrive. Back for breakfast by 9.

meta.title: Uma Sari — About
meta.description: The story of Uma Sari — built slow, on purpose, above the Ayung River in Ubud.

story.label: The Beginning
story.title: A plot of jungle, twelve years of work
body: Uma Sari started as a piece of land above the Ayung River that nobody wanted. The slope was too steep, the access road unpaved, the jungle too thick. In 2012, founder Adi Wijaya saw something else: the most private acre in Ubud.

What followed was twelve years of slow, deliberate building. No investors. No franchise. No rush. Each villa was completed before the next was started. Each material was sourced locally — volcanic stone from Tabanan, teak reclaimed from dismantled joglo homes in Central Java, alang-alang grass from the hillsides nearby.
Uma Sari opened quietly in 2024 with four villas. We added four more in 2025. The final four will be completed by the end of this year.

values.label: How We Think
values.title: Three things we refuse to compromise

items.name: Privacy above all
items.description: No shared pools. No buffet breakfasts. No group activities. Every experience at Uma Sari is private by design, not by upgrade.

items.name: Local always
items.description: Every person who works here lives within 30 minutes of this property. Every ingredient in our kitchen is sourced from Bali.

items.name: Slow by choice
items.description: We will never have more than twelve villas. Growth for its own sake is not something we're interested in.

sustainability.label: Sustainability
sustainability.title: Leave the jungle better than we found it
sustainability.body: Every decision at Uma Sari runs through a single filter: does this leave the land better or worse? We've solar-powered all twelve villas, composted all kitchen waste since day one, and planted 400 trees on adjacent land.

stats.description: Solar powered. Zero grid electricity since 2024.
Trees planted on adjacent land since opening.
Single-use plastics anywhere on the property.
Maximum sourcing radius for all food and materials.
